subject: Databases
title: Polymorphic Associations
description: A micro-lesson that makes Polymorphic Associations usable.

hook.text: Comments on posts, photos, and videos. Three different parents, one comments table. How do you link them?

buildup.text: Polymorphic associations use two columns: commentable_id and commentable_type (post, photo, or video).

discovery.text: Rails' Active Record popularized this pattern. It's flexible and avoids creating three separate comment tables.

twist.text: The database can't enforce foreign keys across multiple tables with one column. Integrity relies on the app.

climax.text: Alternative: separate join tables (post_comments, photo_comments). More tables, but full foreign key support.

punchline.text: Polymorphic is convenient. Separate tables are safer. Pick your trade-off.

quiz.question: What is the main drawback of polymorphic associations?
options: The database cannot enforce foreign key constraints across multiple parent tables
They only work with Rails and no other framework
Polymorphic associations make all queries slower
